name: Brown-Eyed Susan Cake
recipeIngredient: Orange juice, just enough to make easy to spread
Rich milk or cream, to make glossy and easy to spread.
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease and flour 2 9-inch layer pans.
2. Cream the shortening, butter, and sugar until fluffy.
3. Beat eggs in thoroughly.
4. Sift together: flour, baking powder, and salt.
5. Stir in alternately with 1 cup milk.
6. Divide batter into two parts. Into one part, blend: chocolate, baking soda, and milk. Blend into the other part: orange extract, orange rind, and food coloring.
7. Drop the chocolate and orange batters alternately by spoonfuls into prepared pans. Bake at 350°F for 25-30 minutes or until tests done.
Frosting:
1. Heat sweet butter and cream in a saucepan.
2. Stir in confectioner's sugar.
3. Divide frosting into half.
4. To the smaller amount, add the orange rind, orange juice, just enough to make easy to spread.
5. To the larger amount, add: chocolate; melted rich milk or cream, to make glossy and easy to spread.
6. Spread some of the chocolate frosting between layers and the orange frosting around the sides, spreading it to create a scalloped edge on top. Sprinkle grated orange rind and clusters of shaved chocolate over the top of the cake to give the effect of a field of Brown-Eyed Susans!
